rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute-on-chronic critical limb ischaemia with ischaemic rest pain, tissue loss (dry gangrene) and clinical hypoperfusion is a limb-threatening emergency. Telehealth cannot palpate pulses, measure an ABI, or control the pain crisis, so urgent same-day in-person assessment is required — emergency department or urgent vascular (hours, not a routine appointment). Baseline is T4 rather than T5 because the ischaemia is subacute (a window of hours) rather than a minutes-critical arrest; however, it escalates to T5 (000) immediately on signs of acute complete occlusion (the 6 Ps) or systemic sepsis. Given severe intractable pain, exhaustion and living alone, an ambulance/000 disposition is also acceptable (tolerated over-triage).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe worsening of pain with the foot turning blue/black or mottled, going numb, or unable to be moved
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals acute complete arterial occlusion (acute limb ischaemia) — a time-critical emergency where every minute of delay costs viable tissue.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the foot suddenly turns blue or black, goes numb, or you cannot move it, call 000 for an ambulance straight away and say the leg has lost its circulation.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, shivering/rigors, spreading redness up the calf, pus or foul discharge, or feeling generally very unwell
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 or go to ED now
escalation_edges.rationale: Superimposed limb/systemic infection on a necrotic ischaemic wound can progress to sepsis rapidly and needs emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a fever or shivering, the redness spreads up the leg, pus appears, or you feel very unwell, call 000 or go to the emergency department right away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The baseline presentation — non-healing black ankle ulcer with cold pale foot and severe rest pain relieved only by dependency
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — urgent in-person assessment today, do not wait for a routine appointment
escalation_edges.rationale: Limb-threatening ischaemia that cannot be assessed or safely managed remotely requires urgent same-day in-person vascular/ED assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: You need to be seen in person urgently today at an emergency department or by an urgent vascular service — this is a circulation problem a video call can't safely assess or treat.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, housebound, exhausted with a high falls risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange an ambulance or assisted transport rather than self-driving; consider a welfare check, as there is no one at home to help if the pain or the limb suddenly worsens.
Lower threshold to escalate and to arrange transport; higher risk of rapid deterioration, polypharmacy and sedation/falls if opioids are started — reinforces in-person management.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be seen in person urgently today — this is a circulation problem in the leg that a video call cannot safely assess or treat. Go to, or arrange transport to, an emergency department or urgent vascular assessment now; do not wait for a routine appointment. Keep the leg hanging down (not raised) and keep it warm and loosely covered. Do not put any compression bandage or tight stocking on it. Keep the wound clean and dry — do not soak it or pick at the black area.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend ED today, or call your GP for an urgent same-day in-person appointment; call 000 immediately if the foot suddenly worsens.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours, go to the nearest emergency department — do not wait until morning, given the severe pain and the risk to the limb.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Non-healing punched-out lateral-malleolar ulcer with dry black eschar, ischaemic rest pain relieved by dependency, cold pale hairless foot — urgent vascular assessment with ABI and arterial imaging needed; avoid compression and elevation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot confirm pulses, ABI or perfusion, and cannot exclude progression to acute complete occlusion.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as limb-threatening and escalate to urgent same-day in-person assessment, with clear 000 safety-netting for acute deterioration.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassuring the patient, arranging routine wound care/dressings, or a non-urgent referral because the wound is 'chronic'.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Managing as routine chronic wound care or a non-urgent referral, missing limb-threatening ischaemia.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delayed revascularisation leads to progressive gangrene and major amputation; unrelieved pain and exhaustion cause a fall at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent same-day disposition without emphasising the limb-threat or the compression/elevation contraindications — correct urgency but avoidable interim harm if compression/elevation is applied.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling 000/ambulance for a subacute limb threat — tolerated, as ambulance transport is reasonable given severe pain, exhaustion and living alone.
